title: Compositions and methods for producing high secreted yields of recombinant proteins
abstract: The present disclosure relates to methods for producing recombinant proteins, as well as compositions used in and produced by such methods. Specifically, the present disclosure relates to methods for producing high secreted yields of recombinant proteins, and the compositions provided herein include expression constructs, recombinant vectors, and recombinant host cells that comprise polynucleotide sequences encoding proteins operably linked to recombinant secretion signals that comprise the leader peptide of the Î±-mating factor (Î±MF) of Saccharomyces cerevisiae and a non-Î±MF signal peptide.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An expression construct comprising a polynucleotide sequence that encodes a protein operably linked to a recombinant secretion signal, wherein said recombinant secretion signal comprises a leader peptide and a signal peptide, wherein said leader peptide is native pro- aMF(sc) or a functional variant that has an at least 80% amino acid sequence identity to SEQ ID NO: 1, and wherein said signal peptide does not comprise pre-aMF(sc). 2. The expression construct of claim 1 , wherein said functional variant is native pro-aMF(sc) comprising one or two substituted amino acids. 3. The expression construct of claim 1, wherein said functional variant is SEQ ID NO: 2. 4. The expression construct of claim 1, wherein said functional variant has an at least 85%, at least 90%, at least 95%, or at least 99% amino acid sequence identity to SEQ ID NO: 1. 5. The expression construct of claim 1, wherein said signal peptide is selected from Table 1 or is a functional variant that has an at least 80% amino acid sequence identity to a signal peptide selected from Table 1. 6. The expression construct of claim 5, wherein said functional variant has an at least 85%, at least 90% identical, at least 95%, or at least 99% amino acid sequence identity to a signal peptide selected from Table 1. 7. The expression construct of claim 1, wherein said recombinant secretion signal is selected from Table 2 or is a functional variant that has an at least 80% amino acid sequence identity to a recombinant secretion signal selected from Table 2. 8. The expression construct of claim 1, wherein said recombinant secretion signal has an at least 85%, at least 90%, at least 95%, or at least 99% amino acid sequence identity to a recombinant secretion signal selected from Table 2. 9. The expression construct of claim 1 , wherein said polynucleotide sequence is present in multiple copies. 10. The expression construct of claim 1, wherein said polynucleotide sequence is operably linked to a promoter. 11. The expression construct of claim 10, wherein said promoter is a pGCW14 promoter of Pichia pastoris. 12. The expression construct of claim 10, wherein said promoter is a pGAP promoter of Pichia pastoris. 13. The expression construct of claim 10, wherein said promoter is an inducible promoter. 14. The expression construct of claim 1, wherein said protein is a silk or silk-like protein. 15. The expression construct of claim 14, wherein said protein comprises SEQ ID NO: 17. 16. The expression construct of claim 14, wherein said protein comprises multiple copies of SEQ ID NO: 17. 17. A recombinant vector comprising an expression construct of any of claims 1 through 16. 18. A recombinant vector of claim 17, wherein said expression construct is present in multiple copies. 19. A recombinant vector of claim 17, wherein said vector comprises a PARS. 20. A recombinant host cell comprising an expression construct of any of claims 1 through 16. 21. The recombinant host cell of claim 20, wherein said recombinant host cell is a yeast cell. 22. The recombinant host cell of claim 21, wherein said recombinant host cell is a budding yeast cell. 23. The recombinant host cell of claim 21, wherein said recombinant host cell is a methylotrophic yeast cell. 24. The recombinant host cell of claim 22, wherein said recombinant host cell is a Pichia species. 25. The recombinant host cell of claim 23, wherein said recombinant host cell is Pichia pastoris. 26. The recombinant host cell of claim 20, wherein said expression construct is stably integrated within the genome of said recombinant host cell. 27. The recombinant host cell of claim 20, wherein said expression construct is maintained extrachromosomally in said recombinant host cell. 28. The recombinant host cell of claim 20, wherein said recombinant host cell produces a secreted yield of said protein encoded by said polynucleotide sequence comprised in said expression construct of at least 30% by weight of a total yield of said protein produced by said recombinant host cell. 29. A fermentation comprising the recombinant host cell of any one of claims 20 through 28 and a culture medium. 30. The fermentation of claim 29, wherein the fermentation comprises as secreted protein at least 30% by weight of total yield of said protein encoded by said polynucleotide sequence comprised in said expression construct produced by said recombinant host cell. 31. The fermentation of claim 29, wherein the culture medium of the fermentation comprises at least 0.5 g/L of said protein encoded by said polynucleotide sequence comprised in said expression construct. 32. A method for producing a protein, comprising the steps of: a) culturing a recombinant host cell of any of claims 20 through 28 in a culture medium to obtain a fermentation of any of claims 29 through 31 ; and b) extracting said protein from the culture medium.
